Here are all the hardcoded email addresses found in the webapp source code:

| # | File | Line | Email Address |
|---|------|------|---------------|
| 1 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 9 | `alice@example.com` |
| 2 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 10 | `bob@example.com` |
| 3 | `src/UsersController.scala` | 11 | `charlie@example.com` |

All three are located in **`UsersController.scala`**, embedded as hardcoded seed/test data in `User` object literals:
- `User(1, "Alice", "alice@example.com", ...)`
- `User(2, "Bob", "bob@example.com", ...)`
- `User(3, "Charlie", "charlie@example.com", ...)`

These appear to be placeholder/mock users (likely a hardcoded in-memory list). For production, these should be replaced with database-backed user records rather than hardcoded data in source code.
